data.title: Is It Time To Rethink Equinor (OB:EQNR) After This Year’s 54% Share Price Surge?
data.description:
• If you are wondering whether Equinor's share price fairly reflects its potential or is giving you a margin of safety, you are in the right place for a clear valuation checkup.
  • Equinor's shares last closed at NOK 371.8, with a 4.9% return over the past 7 days, an 11.6% decline over 30 days, and returns of 53.9% year to date and 65.9% over the last year.
  • These moves have been set against a steady flow of news around Equinor's role in global energy markets, including its position among major oil and gas producers and its ongoing capital allocation decisions. Taken together, this context can influence how investors weigh both opportunity and risk in the current share price.
  • Simply Wall St currently gives Equinor a valuation score of 1 out of 6. The sections that follow will walk through what different valuation approaches suggest about that score, and then finish with a broader way of thinking about Equinor's value that goes beyond the numbers alone.

Equinor scores just 1/6 on our valuation checks. See what other red flags we found in the full valuation breakdown.

Advertisement

Approach 1: Equinor Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow, or DCF, model projects a company’s future cash flows and then discounts them back to today’s value to estimate what the business could be worth right now.

For Equinor, the model uses a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity approach based on cash flow projections in US$. The latest twelve month free cash flow is about US$7.64b. Analysts provide explicit estimates for the next few years, and beyond that Simply Wall St extrapolates the trend to build a full 10 year path of cash flows, including a projected free cash flow of US$3.41b in 2030.

Discounting this stream of cash flows back to today results in an estimated intrinsic value of US$254.32 per share. Compared with the current share price of NOK 371.8, the DCF output implies the shares are around 46.2% above this intrinsic value. This suggests Equinor may be trading on the expensive side based on this model alone.

Result: OVERVALUED

Our Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests Equinor may be overvalued by 46.2%. Discover 239 high quality undervalued stocks or create your own screener to find better value opportunities.

Head to the Valuation section of our Company Report for more details on how we arrive at this Fair Value for Equinor.

Approach 2: Equinor Price vs Earnings

For a profitable company, the P/E ratio is a useful quick check because it links what you pay for each share to the earnings that support that share. It also reflects what the market is willing to pay for those earnings today.

What counts as a “normal” P/E often comes down to expectations and risk. Higher expected growth and lower perceived risk tend to justify a higher P/E, while lower growth and higher risk usually align with a lower P/E.

Equinor is currently trading on a P/E of 19.80x. This sits above the Oil and Gas industry average P/E of 15.67x, while the peer group average is much higher at 60.23x. To go a step further, Simply Wall St calculates a proprietary “Fair Ratio” for Equinor of 12.36x. This is the P/E that would be expected given factors such as its earnings profile, industry, profit margins, market cap and risk characteristics.

This Fair Ratio can be more informative than a simple peer or industry comparison because it adjusts for those fundamentals rather than just lining Equinor up against broad groups. Comparing 19.80x with a Fair Ratio of 12.36x points to Equinor trading above that modelled fair level.

Result: OVERVALUED

P/E ratios tell one story, but what if the real opportunity lies elsewhere? Start investing in legacies, not executives. Discover our 96 top founder-led companies.

Upgrade Your Decision Making: Choose your Equinor Narrative

Earlier it was mentioned that there is an even better way to understand valuation. Narratives bring this to life by letting you attach a clear story about Equinor, such as your view on future revenue, earnings and margins, to a financial forecast and then to a Fair Value that you can compare directly with the current share price.

On Simply Wall St's Community page, Narratives are available as an easy tool used by millions of investors. They link your view of Equinor's role in global energy, its oil and gas exposure and its renewables ambitions to specific assumptions like a Fair Value around NOK 239.5 at the bearish end or roughly NOK 416.7 at the bullish end, so you can see how different beliefs map to different outcomes.

Because Narratives update when new news, analyst targets or earnings are added to the platform, you can quickly see when your Fair Value and the current market price start to differ in a way that might prompt you to review whether Equinor still fits your portfolio or whether the story you believe in has shifted.

For Equinor however we will make it really easy for you with previews of two leading Equinor Narratives:

🐂 Equinor Bull Case

Fair value in this bullish narrative: NOK 416.72 per share

Current price vs this fair value: around 10.8% below the narrative fair value based on the last close of NOK 371.8

Revenue growth assumption: 2.62% a year

  • Analysts in this camp lean on stronger cash flow support from projects like Johan Sverdrup and Johan Castberg, plus rapid capital shifts into higher return assets in Brazil, the U.S., and selected renewables.
  • The narrative expects higher earnings and margin improvement, helped by aggressive natural gas positioning and efficiency gains, while still flagging exposure to oil and gas demand, regulation, and ESG pressures.
  • To line up with this view, you would need to be comfortable with higher earnings, a P/E of 11.1x on 2029 earnings and a fair value around NOK 416.72.

🐻 Equinor Bear Case

Fair value in this bearish narrative: NOK 239.50 per share

Current price vs this fair value: around 55.2% above the narrative fair value based on the last close of NOK 371.8

Revenue growth assumption: 2.28% annual decline

  • Here the focus sits on faster decarbonization, tighter climate rules, and decommissioning costs, which together could weigh on long term oil and gas revenues and legacy asset values.
  • This view also highlights risks around offshore wind and other renewables projects, including impairments, regulatory changes, and cost overruns that could pressure future earnings.
  • To agree with this narrative, you would need to assume lower revenue over time, more modest margin improvement, a P/E of 10.1x on 2029 earnings, and a fair value closer to NOK 239.50.

These two Equinor Narratives frame the current share price against very different assumptions about future energy demand, regulation, and project returns. data.title: ATI (ATI) Net Margin Of 8.8% Tests Bullish Profit Expansion Narrative
data.description:
ATI (ATI) has opened Q1 2026 earnings season with Q4 2025 revenue of US$1.2 billion and basic EPS of US$0.71, setting the stage against a current share price of US$155.46 as investors focus on how these results fit into the company’s recent earnings trajectory. Over the past year, quarterly revenue has ranged from US$1.1 billion to US$1.2 billion, while basic EPS has moved between US$0.64 and US$0.80. This provides a clear view of how the top and bottom line have tracked together across recent reporting periods. With trailing twelve month basic EPS at US$2.92 and net margin at 8.8%, attention now turns to whether ATI’s profitability profile is giving investors enough comfort around the sustainability of these earnings.

See our full analysis for ATI.

With the headline numbers on the table, the next step is to see how ATI’s latest earnings line up with the key market narratives on growth, margins, and risk that have developed over the past few years.

See what the community is saying about ATI

Advertisement

Net margin at 8.8% with four quarter EPS above US$0.68

  • Across the last four reported quarters, ATI’s basic EPS sat between US$0.68 and US$0.80, while trailing net margin was 8.8% compared with 8.4% a year earlier, on trailing twelve month net income of US$404.3 million from US$4.6b in revenue.
  • Consensus narrative expects higher margin businesses to matter more over time, and the current 8.8% net margin gives a concrete starting point to test that view.
    • Analysts in the balanced view are assuming net margin can move from 8.8% to 13.5%, supported by contracts that tie pricing to inflation and focus on higher value alloys.
    • The fact that recent quarterly net income has been in the US$96.6 million to US$110 million range shows the earnings base that needs to expand for that margin step up to play out.

Earnings growth slowing versus 5 year pace

  • ATI’s 1 year earnings growth of 9.9% sits well below its 5 year earnings growth rate of 65.1% per year, even though trailing twelve month EPS is US$2.92 on US$404.3 million of net income.
  • Bulls argue the slower recent growth is a pause before another leg up in profits, but the numbers set a high bar for that optimism.
    • The bullish narrative looks for earnings to reach US$861.9 million, which is more than double the current US$404.3 million level, and assumes revenue grows 9.4% a year, above the consensus 7.7% forecast.
    • At the same time, bullish analysts expect margins to rise from 8.8% to 14.4%, so the current 9.9% earnings growth rate and single digit margin leave a lot of progress to be made to match that path.
Have a look at how the optimistic case stacks up against the actual earnings path so far: 🐂 ATI Bull Case.

Premium 52.5x P/E with DCF fair value below price

  • ATI is trading on a trailing P/E of 52.5x at a share price of US$155.46, compared with a peer average of 41.3x and an Aerospace & Defense industry average of 35.5x, while the provided DCF fair value is US$140.59.
  • Bears focus on this valuation gap, and the current earnings profile gives some support to their caution.
    • The bearish narrative assumes earnings can climb from around US$398.7 million to US$612.9 million, yet the share price already sits above the DCF fair value of US$140.59, which leaves less room if growth follows the lower 4.3% revenue path they outline.
    • With earnings forecast to grow 16.3% a year from the current US$2.92 EPS base and revenue forecast at 7.7% a year, skeptics question whether that growth and an 8.8% margin are enough to support a 52.5x P/E against cheaper peers.
If you want to see why some investors question paying a premium multiple here, check the cautious case next: 🐻 ATI Bear Case.

data.title: Viavi Solutions (VIAV) Q3 Profit Challenges Bearish Narratives On Persistent Losses
data.description:
Viavi Solutions (VIAV) just posted Q3 2026 results with revenue of US$406.8 million and basic EPS of US$0.03, while on a trailing twelve month basis revenue stands at US$1.4 billion with a basic EPS loss of US$0.24. Over recent quarters, the company has seen revenue move from US$270.8 million in Q2 2025 to US$406.8 million in Q3 2026, alongside basic EPS shifting from US$0.04 in Q2 2025 to US$0.03 in the latest quarter and swinging between profits and losses in the periods in between. This puts the spotlight firmly on how quickly margins can stabilise from here.

See our full analysis for Viavi Solutions.

With the headline numbers on the table, the next step is to see how this earnings print lines up with the most common narratives around Viavi Solutions, highlighting where the data supports the story and where it pushes back.

See what the community is saying about Viavi Solutions

Advertisement

Revenue climbs toward US$1.4b run-rate

  • On a trailing twelve month basis, Viavi has generated US$1.4b of revenue, up from US$1.0b a year earlier, with quarterly revenue moving from US$270.8 million in Q2 2025 to US$406.8 million in Q3 2026.
  • Supporters of the bullish narrative point to this revenue ramp as backing long term data center and fiber upgrade themes. However, the trend also has to carry the weight of current unprofitability:
    • Bulls highlight forecasts that revenue could grow around 18.3% per year and that structural demand for high speed optical test equipment and aerospace and defense solutions can sustain that trajectory.
    • Against that, trailing twelve month net income is still a loss of US$55.1 million and EPS over the same period is a loss of US$0.24, so the stronger top line has not yet translated into consistent profits.
Supporters who want to see how the optimistic case lines up with detailed forecasts and segment drivers can go deeper into the bull argument in 🐂 Viavi Solutions Bull Case.

  • Q3 2026 delivered net income of US$6.4 million and basic EPS of roughly US$0.03, yet over the last twelve months the company still recorded a net loss of US$55.1 million and a basic EPS loss of US$0.24.
  • Skeptics in the bearish camp focus on this profit gap when they argue that near term earnings risk remains high, even with growth stories in place:
    • Bears point out that the company has been unprofitable on a trailing basis and that losses have grown at about 51.3% per year over the past five years, which they see as a warning against leaning too hard on a single profitable quarter.
    • They also flag that interest coverage is weak, with earnings not comfortably covering interest expense. The path from a small Q3 profit to sustained, debt friendly earnings is therefore a key point of pressure for the cautious view.
